package handler_test

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
)

// Positive scenario for tests_assert_raw_json_strings_without_response_dto.
// Rule intent: Brittle raw-JSON string assertions when typed response DTOs exist.

type UserResponse struct {
	ID    string `json:"id"`
	Name  string `json:"name"`
	Email string `json:"email"`
}

func GetUserHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, UserResponse{
		ID:    "123",
		Name:  "John",
		Email: "john@example.com",
	})
}

func TestGetUserHandler_AssertsRawJSON(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	c, _ := gin.CreateTestContext(w)

	c.Request = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/users/123", nil)
	GetUserHandler(c)

	body := w.Body.String()

	// Anti-pattern: asserting raw JSON strings instead of unmarshaling into a typed DTO
	assert.Contains(t, body, `"name":"John"`)
	assert.Contains(t, body, `"email":"john@example.com"`)
	assert.Contains(t, body, `"id":"123"`)
	assert.True(t, strings.Contains(body, `"name":`) && strings.Contains(body, `"John"`))
}

func TestCreateUserHandler_AssertsRawJSON(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	c, _ := gin.CreateTestContext(w)

	c.Request = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/users", strings.NewReader(`{"name":"Jane"}`))
	c.Request.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")

	body := w.Body.String()

	// Anti-pattern: raw substring matching on JSON instead of DTO-based assertions
	assert.True(t, strings.Contains(body, `"message"`))
	assert.Contains(t, body, `"created"`)
}
